title: War Risk for Businesses Will Mean Higher Prices No Matter What Happens
summary: Doing business in a more dangerous and unpredictable world will cost more, pushing up the price of everything from food to electronics.

title: U.S. Strikes Hit Greater Tunb Island in Strait of Hormuz
summary: The U.S. military said on Wednesday it had attacked Iranian cruise missile storage and launch sites on Greater Tunb, one of several small islands in the Strait of Hormuz subject to a decades-old territorial dispute.

title: F.B.I. Searches Houston ICE Shooting Van for Drugs
summary: Federal investigators believe illegal drugs were inside the vehicle, though the immigration agents involved in the encounter do not appear to have known that.

title: Trump Pressures ICE to Resume Traffic Stops After They Were Halted Over Fatal Shootings
summary: Immigration and Customs Enforcement had ordered its officers to halt most vehicle stops across the country after they shot two people over the past week.

title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
summary: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
summary: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.

title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
summary: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.

title: House G.O.P. Releases Budget to Unlock $95 Billion for Iran War and SAVE Act
summary: The plan faces a rocky path amid Republican divisions and would pave the way for votes months before the midterm elections to fund a war that polls show is deeply unpopular.

title: Our Top Editor on Subpoenas of Times Journalists
summary: The Trump administration issued subpoenas to reporters who wrote about security concerns involving the new Air Force One. Joe Kahn, the executive editor of The New York Times, responds to what he calls a “naked attempt to intimidate” the newspaper.

title: Treasury to Begin Minting $1 Gold Trump Coins Featuring President’s Face
summary: Critics question whether the coins are legal and argue that President Trump is using the currency to celebrate himself.

title: He Thought He Had to Break Out of Brooklyn. Instead, He Celebrates It.
summary: As a child, Michael McLeer wanted to be like John Travolta in “Saturday Night Fever” and leave the neighborhood. A tragedy changed his mind.

title: Inside Maine Democrats’ Unusual, Rushed Search for a New Senate Nominee
summary: Democratic candidates are hastily building bare-bones campaigns and forgoing traditional outreach like TV ads in a sprint to appeal to party delegates, rather than Maine voters.

title: Elon Musk Funds Vivek Ramaswamy’s Bid for Ohio Governor Despite Their DOGE Clashes
summary: Elon Musk gave $5 million to back Vivek Ramaswamy’s campaign for Ohio governor, seemingly moving past tensions around the effort to downsize the federal work force.

title: Biden Will Release Memoir Just After the November Midterms
summary: The former president announced that the book, “Promise Me, America,” will come out on Nov. 17, and will cover the tumultuous events of his presidency as well as his re-election bid.

title: Should Epstein’s Friends Be Punished? An Elite Harvard Club Is Torn.
summary: At Harvard, students, alumni and professors debate whether a storied campus group should cut ties with one of Jeffrey Epstein’s closest associates, and whether Harvard itself should do the same.

title: Goldman’s Former Top Lawyer Calls Epstein a ‘Masterful Liar’
summary: Kathryn Ruemmler, the former general counsel of Goldman Sachs, testified on Wednesday in a closed-door congressional hearing investigating Jeffery Epstein.

title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
summary: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.

title: New Jersey Couple Aids Meteorite Discovery After it Crashes Through Their Roof
summary: A New Jersey couple helped scientists uncover the makeup of an unusual asteroid after it fell into their bedroom.

title: Flash Flood Emergency Is Issued in Texas After Days of Pounding Rain
summary: Roads were turning to rivers on Wednesday as emergency officials in towns around San Antonio raced to rescue people who had been stranded.

title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
summary: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.

title: Canada Wildfire Air Pollution and Heat Wave Combo Pose Heightened Health Risks
summary: Days that are both extremely hot and polluted come with higher risks of respiratory ailments and other health risks.

title: How Worried Should You Be About Cyclospora?
summary: Don’t stop eating fruits and vegetables.

title: When Originalism Jumped the Shark
summary: Has the court learned nothing?

title: Fatal Encounters
summary: We look at shootings by federal immigration agents.

title: Small Tribes Say They Lack the Clout to Secure Federal Recognition
summary: The federal government can confer health care, education, housing and casino rights, but for small bands like many in the Pacific Northwest, recognition takes political muscle they don’t have.
